name: GPT-5.5
official_claims: Matches GPT-5.4 speed while delivering higher intelligence
82.7% on Terminal-Bench 2.0 (vs Claude Opus 4.7's 69.4%)
SWE-Bench Pro: 58.6%, OSWorld-Verified: 78.7%
60% fewer hallucinations vs GPT-5.4
Uses significantly fewer tokens to complete tasks (higher efficiency)
State-of-the-art on agentic coding, computer use, and scientific research
external_validation_summary: BenchLM ranks GPT-5.5 #3 overall (91/100) among 115 models with Arena Elo 1475. Leads on Terminal-Bench 2.0 by 13+ points over nearest competitor. Strong independent confirmation from multiple benchmark trackers.
real_change_notes: Significant agentic coding leap; beats Claude Opus 4.7 on terminal/computer-use tasks. More token-efficient than predecessors. Available in ChatGPT and Codex; API deployment announced.
selection_impact_notes: Likely to become default for agentic coding pipelines where terminal-use and multi-step tasks dominate. Relevant replacement consideration vs Claude Opus 4.7 for coding workflows.
price_speed_cost_notes: Pricing not yet disclosed for API; released to Plus/Pro/Business/Enterprise ChatGPT. Token efficiency improvement means lower effective cost per task.
app_api_workflow_notes: Available in ChatGPT and Codex at launch; API availability announced as coming soon. Tight integration with OpenAI Codex for agentic coding.
new_use_cases: Long-horizon agentic coding (20-hour senior dev tasks)
Computer use / desktop automation
Scientific research automation
Complex multi-step data analysis and document generation
ecosystem_echo: Positioned as the top agentic coding model, directly competing with Claude Opus 4.7 for developer workflows. Significant industry attention.
durability_notes: Major frontier release; likely to hold top position for 2-3 months before next update cycle.
risk_caveat: API pricing not yet confirmed. System card notes red-teaming concerns for cybersecurity/biology misuse. Slightly outside Apr 29 – May 6 window (released Apr 23) but GPT-5.5 Instant (May 5) is within window.

good: Developers praising agentic task completion quality
Token efficiency praised (fewer tokens per task)
bad: API not yet available at launch (ChatGPT-only initially)

one_line_judgment: OpenAI's strongest agentic coding model yet, leading Terminal-Bench 2.0 at 82.7% and delivering higher intelligence at GPT-5.4 speeds.
relevance_to_us: High — directly relevant for AI-native creative tool workflows requiring agentic coding, computer use, and multi-step task execution. Strong candidate for skill/agent infrastructure backbone.

name: GPT-5.5 Instant
official_claims: 52.5% fewer hallucinated claims than GPT-5.3 Instant on high-stakes topics (medicine, law)
AIME 2025 math: 81.2 (vs 65.4 for GPT-5.3 Instant)
MMMU-Pro: 76 (vs 69.2 for GPT-5.3 Instant)
Enhanced image analysis and STEM question answering
Better personalization using past chats, memories, files, and Gmail
New Memory Sources feature shows what context shaped each response
external_validation_summary: TechCrunch and VentureBeat confirmed launch on May 5. Benchmark improvements independently noted. Memory Sources transparency feature widely covered.
real_change_notes: New default ChatGPT model as of May 5. Meaningful hallucination reduction. Memory Sources adds auditability. Within the April 29 – May 6 collection window.
selection_impact_notes: Replaces GPT-5.3 Instant as default for 200M+ ChatGPT users. Personalization improvements and memory sources are meaningful UX differentiator.
price_speed_cost_notes: Same pricing tier as predecessor. GPT-5.3 Instant available to paid users for 3 more months before retirement.
app_api_workflow_notes: Available via API as 'chat-latest'. Memory Sources UI in ChatGPT web/mobile (mobile rollout pending).
new_use_cases: High-stakes Q&A with reduced hallucination (legal, medical)
Personalized contextual assistance using multi-source memory
STEM and image analysis tasks
business_model: Free tier (default model) + Plus/Pro subscription + API pay-per-token
ecosystem_echo: Signals OpenAI's push toward persistent memory and personalization as core ChatGPT differentiator. Etsy app integration launched same day shows ecosystem momentum.
durability_notes: Default model replacement; impact is systemic across all ChatGPT users.
risk_caveat: Memory Sources described as 'not full auditability yet' — partial transparency only.

good: Hallucination reduction on sensitive topics well-received
Memory transparency (Sources) seen as useful trust feature
bad: Memory Sources doesn't show all factors that shaped an answer

one_line_judgment: ChatGPT's new default model with 52.5% fewer hallucinations and a new memory sources transparency layer — within collection window (May 5).
relevance_to_us: Medium-high — default model upgrade affects all ChatGPT integrations; memory + personalization patterns relevant for creative tool design.

name: Claude Opus 4.7
official_claims: SWE-bench Verified: 87.6% (up from 80.8% for Opus 4.6)
GPQA Diamond: 94.2%
GDPval-AA: 1,753 Elo — leads GPT-5.4 and Gemini 3.1 Pro on knowledge work
High-resolution vision: images up to 2,576px (3.3x more than Opus 4.6), 98.5% visual-acuity
New xhigh effort level for deeper reasoning
Task budgets (beta) for token-bounded agentic loops
Self-verification: writes and runs tests before reporting results
35% fewer output tokens than Opus 4.6 while scoring 4 points higher on AI Index
external_validation_summary: Artificial Analysis confirms 35% fewer output tokens with higher quality. VentureBeat validates 'narrowly retaking lead for most powerful GA model'. Multiple independent benchmark trackers confirm SWE-bench 87.6%.
real_change_notes: Major capability jump on SWE-bench (+6.8pp), vision (3.3x resolution), and self-verification. Also adds Managed Agents Memory (Apr 23 — within window) and Rate Limits API (Apr 24 — within window).
selection_impact_notes: Best GA model for software engineering and knowledge work as of collection date. Vision improvements directly relevant for creative/design AI tools requiring image understanding.
price_speed_cost_notes: Unchanged at $5/$25 per million input/output tokens. 35% fewer output tokens = ~26% cost reduction in practice vs Opus 4.6.
app_api_workflow_notes: Available via Anthropic API, Amazon Bedrock, Google Cloud Vertex AI, Microsoft Foundry. New xhigh effort level and task budgets add workflow control. Managed Agents Memory (beta) launched Apr 23.
new_use_cases: High-resolution image analysis for creative tools (mockups, screenshots, design review)
Long agentic coding loops with self-verification
Multi-session work with file-system memory
Token-bounded agentic task completion with budgets
business_model: API pay-per-token ($5/$25 per M tokens) + Claude.ai subscription tiers
ecosystem_echo: Leading frontier model on SWE-bench and knowledge work. Adobe Firefly AI Assistant connector integrates Claude (Apr 28). Strong enterprise adoption via Bedrock/Vertex.
durability_notes: Top-of-class for 1-2 months; GPT-5.5 leads on terminal/computer-use tasks but Opus 4.7 leads on SWE-bench and knowledge work. Both will likely hold dual top positions.
risk_caveat: Released Apr 16 — 13 days before collection window start. Included as it remains the leading GA model and Claude platform updates (Managed Agents Memory Apr 23, Rate Limits API Apr 24) fall within window.

good: Self-verification feature widely praised for reducing coding errors
Vision resolution upgrade significant for design/image tasks
xhigh effort level gives finer control for expensive reasoning tasks
bad: API breaking changes from Opus 4.6 require migration
Still behind GPT-5.5 on terminal-bench and computer use

one_line_judgment: Anthropic's flagship retakes #1 SWE-bench at 87.6% with self-verification, 3.3x vision resolution, and 35% token efficiency gain.
relevance_to_us: Very high — top model for coding, vision tasks (creative tool images), and agentic loops. Self-verification and task budgets are directly applicable to skill/workflow systems.

name: Grok 4.3 + Voice APIs
official_claims: Grok 4.3: built-in persistent reasoning, 1M token context, multimodal (text+image+video)
Aggressive pricing: $1.25/$2.50 per million input/output tokens (vs Grok 4.2's $2/$6)
Native document generation (PDFs, spreadsheets, PowerPoint)
Voice APIs: STT 5.0% entity error rate (vs ElevenLabs 12%, Deepgram 13.5%)
TTS: 5 voices, 20+ languages, expressive speech tags ([laugh], [sigh], [whisper])
Custom Voice cloning from short recordings (launched Apr 30)
Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0: enterprise voice agent for customer support (Apr 23)
external_validation_summary: VentureBeat and TechSifted confirm pricing and feature details. Voice API error rates cited from xAI's own benchmarks vs competitors — independent validation pending.
real_change_notes: 50%+ price cut vs Grok 4.2. Voice APIs and custom voice cloning are new product surface. Full Grok 4.3 rollout expected mid-to-late May 2026.
selection_impact_notes: Lowest priced 1M-context frontier model. Voice APIs (STT+TTS) open new interaction patterns for voice-enabled creative tools. Custom voice cloning is directly relevant for creative/media applications.
price_speed_cost_notes: $1.25/$2.50 per M tokens — significantly cheaper than GPT-5.5 (TBD) and Claude Opus 4.7 ($5/$25). STT: $0.10/hr batch, $0.20/hr streaming. TTS: $4.20 per 1M characters.
app_api_workflow_notes: Available via xAI API and OpenRouter. Beta for SuperGrok Heavy ($300/mo) subscribers; wider rollout May 2026. Voice APIs are standalone REST endpoints.
new_use_cases: Voice-enabled creative tool interfaces
Custom voice cloning for content creation
Low-cost 1M-context document analysis
Native presentation/slide generation
Enterprise voice agents for support/sales
business_model: API pay-per-token + SuperGrok subscription ($300/mo for heavy) + Grok on X platform
ecosystem_echo: Aggressive pricing pressures industry; integration with X platform and Tesla/Starlink infrastructure signals unique distribution. Custom Voices launched Apr 30 (within collection window).
durability_notes: Price leadership is durable advantage if maintained. Full rollout within collection window's tail.
risk_caveat: Voice API error benchmarks self-reported. Beta-only for highest capabilities (full rollout pending). Some features (video understanding) not yet widely available.

good: Aggressive pricing widely praised by developers
Voice quality (custom cloning) noted as impressive in early reviews
bad: Still in SuperGrok Heavy beta ($300/mo) for full Grok 4.3
Independent voice API benchmark validation lacking

one_line_judgment: xAI cuts Grok 4.3 price by 50%+ while launching a voice cloning suite — the cheapest 1M-context frontier model with the industry's first integrated STT+TTS+cloning stack.
relevance_to_us: High — price leadership and voice cloning APIs are directly relevant for creative tool builders. Custom voices enable new product forms in audio/media creation.

name: Mistral Medium 3.5
official_claims: 128B parameter dense model (not MoE), 256K context window
SWE-Bench Verified: 77.6%
τ³-Telecom agentic benchmark: 91.4%
Self-hostable on 4 GPUs
Configurable reasoning effort per request
Open weights under modified MIT license
Default model in Vibe CLI and Le Chat
external_validation_summary: InfoQ and multiple outlets confirm release and benchmark figures. Open weights on Hugging Face verifiable. SWE-Bench 77.6% is competitive but below Claude Opus 4.7 (87.6%) and GPT-5.5.
real_change_notes: First single-model combining instruction-following, reasoning, coding, and vision (vs separate specialized models). Open weights at frontier-competitive quality. Within collection window (May 2).
selection_impact_notes: Self-hostable frontier-competitive model changes economics for teams that want control over their inference. Vibe remote agents show practical agentic coding use case.
price_speed_cost_notes: $1.50/$7.50 per million input/output tokens via Mistral API. Can self-host on 4 GPUs — potentially $0 marginal cost for teams with GPU infrastructure.
app_api_workflow_notes: Available via Mistral API, Hugging Face, NVIDIA Build. Powers Vibe CLI remote agents and Le Chat Work Mode. Integrates with GitHub, Linear, Jira, Sentry, Slack, Teams.
new_use_cases: Self-hosted agentic coding pipelines
Remote cloud coding agents (Vibe)
Multi-tool work orchestration (Le Chat Work Mode)
Vision tasks with reasoning at self-hostable scale
business_model: API pay-per-token ($1.50/$7.50) + open weights (self-host) + Le Chat subscription
ecosystem_echo: Open weights strategy differentiates from GPT-5.5/Claude. Fills frontier-competitive open-weight gap left by DeepSeek V4's large hardware requirements.
durability_notes: Open-weight release creates lasting ecosystem effect; downstream fine-tuning and hosting will extend relevance.
risk_caveat: Modified MIT license (not fully permissive — check commercial terms). SWE-Bench 77.6% is good but not frontier-leading.

good: 4-GPU self-hosting requirement is accessible for small teams
Unified model (no need to route between specialist models) praised
bad: SWE-Bench below Claude Opus 4.7 and GPT-5.5
Modified MIT license terms need review for commercial use

one_line_judgment: Mistral's 128B open-weight model unifies reasoning, coding, and vision on 4 GPUs — the most accessible self-hostable frontier model, shipping with cloud coding agents on May 2.
relevance_to_us: High — self-hostable at 4 GPUs opens private deployment option for creative tool pipelines. Vibe remote agents pattern is directly applicable to skill/workflow infra.

name: DeepSeek V4
official_claims: DeepSeek-V4-Pro: 1.6T total params, 49B active/token, 1M context, 384K output
DeepSeek-V4-Flash: 284B total params, 13B active/token, 1M context
Hybrid attention (CSA+HCA): 27% of V3.2 FLOPs for Pro, 10% for Flash
KV cache: ~2% the size of grouped query attention systems
MIT license open-source release
external_validation_summary: HuggingFace blog and NVIDIA confirm technical details. Open weights on Hugging Face verifiable. Efficiency claims (FLOP reduction, KV cache) are architectural — verifiable by inference practitioners.
real_change_notes: Massive efficiency breakthrough: 1M-context MoE at a fraction of predecessor FLOPs. Opens 1M-context inference to teams without H100 clusters. Released Apr 24 — borderline outside window.
selection_impact_notes: If FLOP/KV efficiency holds, DeepSeek V4 Pro could enable 1M-context inference on smaller GPU clusters. Flash variant is accessible for medium-scale teams.
price_speed_cost_notes: API via api.deepseek.com (pricing not confirmed in sources). Open weights allow self-hosting with dramatically reduced hardware vs V3/V3.2.
app_api_workflow_notes: Available on DeepSeek API, Hugging Face, chat.deepseek.com. V3/V3.2 API retire July 24, 2026.
new_use_cases: 1M-context document analysis on smaller GPU fleets
Long-context creative project management (entire codebases, scripts, storyboards)
Cost-efficient agentic inference at scale
business_model: API pay-per-token + open weights (MIT license, self-host)
ecosystem_echo: China-origin open-source model maintains competitive pressure on US labs. NVIDIA partnership validates inference efficiency claims.
durability_notes: MIT license + massive efficiency gain = durable ecosystem impact. Open-weight release will be fine-tuned and hosted by many downstream providers.
risk_caveat: Released Apr 24 — just outside collection window. Flagged as 'Preview' — pricing and routing may still shift. Very large model (865GB on-disk for Pro) limits accessibility.

good: Efficiency breakthrough widely celebrated in open-source community
1M context at 2% KV cache size of standard GQA is extraordinary
bad: 865GB on-disk for Pro requires massive storage
Preview status means production use is uncertain

one_line_judgment: DeepSeek V4 brings 1M-context MoE inference at 27% of predecessor FLOPs under MIT license — the open-weight efficiency benchmark for 2026.
relevance_to_us: Medium-high — 1M context + efficiency matters for long-form creative workflows. Open-weight and MIT license relevant for privacy-sensitive creative tool deployments.

name: MiniMax M2.7
official_claims: 230B total params, 10B active/token (sparse MoE), 204,800 context
SWE-Pro: 56.22% (near GPT-5.3-Codex performance)
VIBE-Pro: 55.6%, Terminal Bench 2: 57.0%
GDPval-AA: 1,495 Elo (highest among open-source for office work)
97% skill adherence while working with 40+ complex skills (>2,000 tokens each)
Self-evolution: model participated in its own post-training
Agent Teams for multi-agent collaboration
external_validation_summary: NVIDIA NIM availability and blog post validates technical specs. Open-source on Hugging Face verifiable. r/LocalLLaMA noted M2.7 as significant with 470 points/151 comments in April.
real_change_notes: Released Apr 11 — outside collection window. Notable for self-evolution claims and highest GDPval-AA (office work) among open-source models. Relevant for creative tool teams doing complex multi-tool orchestration.
selection_impact_notes: 97% skill adherence at 40+ skills is directly relevant for skill system design. Self-evolution post-training is a novel architectural claim worth watching.
price_speed_cost_notes: Available via NVIDIA NIM and Hugging Face — pricing per hosting provider. Relatively accessible MoE with 10B active params.
app_api_workflow_notes: NVIDIA NIM microservice, Hugging Face. Supports Agent Teams and dynamic tool search for agentic workflows.
new_use_cases: High-skill-count agentic orchestration (40+ skills)
Office document creation and editing at scale
Self-improving agent systems (post-training via self-evolution)
business_model: API via NVIDIA NIM + open weights on Hugging Face
ecosystem_echo: Strong r/LocalLLaMA community reception. NVIDIA partnership signals mainstream adoption. Self-evolution claim is a first in open-source.
durability_notes: Self-evolution and skill adherence claims set a benchmark others will aim to beat; watch for follow-up M2.8 or successor.
risk_caveat: Released Apr 11 — 18 days outside window. Self-evolution claims are novel and need longer-term validation. Office work ELO benchmark is Minimax's own measure.

good: r/LocalLLaMA: '470 points, 151 comments' reception — very positive
Office task performance praised for Excel/PowerPoint/Word fidelity
bad: Self-evolution claims not yet independently verified
GDPval-AA benchmark is proprietary to MiniMax

one_line_judgment: MiniMax M2.7 is the first open-source model to self-evolve during training and leads open-source office work benchmarks — strong signal for skill system architecture.
relevance_to_us: Medium — skill adherence research and self-evolution pattern directly informing our skill system design. Less urgent given Apr 11 release date.

name: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano Omni
official_claims: Omni-modal: text, images, documents, audio, video understanding in one model
Top rankings on multiple multimodal leaderboards
Designed for document analysis, image reasoning, ASR, audio-video understanding, and computer use
Long-context multimodal intelligence for agents
Released April 28 (borderline in window)
external_validation_summary: HuggingFace blog (official NVIDIA post) confirms capabilities. Transformers library v5.8.0 added native support. Leaderboard positions not independently verified in sources.
real_change_notes: Omni-modal agent model from NVIDIA — positions as infrastructure-level model for document+audio+video agentic pipelines. Released Apr 28 — 1 day before window.
selection_impact_notes: Omni-modal document+audio+video in one model reduces pipeline complexity for creative tools handling mixed media inputs.
price_speed_cost_notes: Available via NVIDIA NIM and Hugging Face. Pricing per NIM endpoint (typically compute-based).
app_api_workflow_notes: NVIDIA NIM microservice + Hugging Face Transformers native support.
new_use_cases: Multi-modal creative asset analysis (documents, audio, video in one pass)
Agent computer use with document and audio understanding
Omni-modal RAG pipelines for creative archives
business_model: NVIDIA NIM enterprise compute + open weights
ecosystem_echo: NVIDIA's push into foundation model space signals GPU vendor vertical integration.
durability_notes: NVIDIA's distribution and NIM infrastructure ensures wide deployment.
risk_caveat: Benchmark positions described but not fully cited in available sources. One day outside collection window.
one_line_judgment: NVIDIA's omni-modal agent model handles documents, audio, and video in one inference call — reduces pipeline complexity for creative multi-media AI applications.
relevance_to_us: Medium — omni-modal pipeline pattern relevant for creative tools ingesting mixed media. Less frontier-critical than GPT-5.5/Claude Opus 4.7.

name: Gemini API Multimodal File Search (Gemini Embedding 2)
official_claims: File Search now multimodal: text+image RAG in one API
Powered by Gemini Embedding 2 model
Custom metadata filtering for unstructured data organization
Page-level citations for improved transparency and grounding
Released May 5, 2026 — within window
external_validation_summary: Official Google blog post confirms. Gemini Embedding 2 powering the feature is the key technical signal — embedding model upgrade enables multimodal retrieval.
real_change_notes: Within collection window (May 5). Enables multimodal RAG without separate embedding pipelines. Reduces developer complexity for multi-format document search.
selection_impact_notes: Relevant for creative tool builders using Gemini API for document+image RAG (design assets, visual references, mixed-media archives).
price_speed_cost_notes: Gemini API pricing applies. Embedding 2 pricing not separately listed.
app_api_workflow_notes: REST API via Gemini API. Custom metadata filtering enables structured retrieval from unstructured visual+text content.
new_use_cases: Multimodal creative asset retrieval (search images and text together)
Visual design archive search
Mixed-format document RAG for product catalogs, design systems
business_model: Gemini API pay-per-use via Google Cloud
ecosystem_echo: Signals Google's push to make Gemini the default embedding+RAG stack for enterprise developers.
durability_notes: API capability additions are sticky — once developers build on multimodal File Search, switching cost is high.
risk_caveat: Limited third-party validation available; Google-sourced only. Not a model release per se — API/embedding model upgrade.
one_line_judgment: Google's Gemini API adds multimodal file search (text+image RAG) on May 5, powered by Gemini Embedding 2 — directly useful for creative asset retrieval pipelines.
relevance_to_us: Medium-high — multimodal RAG API directly useful for creative tools that need to search visual + text content together.
